How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-06-24 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Detects tickers flagged by two or more screeners within one trading day, with edge from validated combo overlap ranked by score.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

HYMC (244) leads the board on BREAKOUT, MA_CROSS, and SMART_MONEY_SURGE while DD (243) pairs BREAKOUT and PULLBACK with VOLUME_LEADER at nearly the same score, but neither fires a validated combo, which cuts conviction sharply. ABSI (173) is the one name worth prioritizing: four screeners including BLOCK_TRADERS and SHORT_SQUEEZE alongside BREAKOUT and PULLBACK suggests institutional flow into a squeeze setup, a combination that at least gestures at the SMART_FLOW-type signal quality the validated combos reward. Score distribution is broad from 95 to 244 across 10 named names (20 total overlaps per stats), with a tight cluster at the top in HYMC and DD and a clean drop after AMAT at 214, making this a top-heavy batch rather than a high-conviction broad wave. NEUTRAL macro at 54.9 neither endorses nor kills the tape, which argues for name-specific selectivity over pressing size. First look is ABSI at low-to-medium conviction, contingent on confirming the block flow is directional rather than a hedge.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: CAT (score 7, target ~$868.23 (DD20d -11.6%)).

2 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 1 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional intent via unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day open-interest delta, filtered through EMA8/21 trend structure to front-run directional moves.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $COIN $CORZ $TEVA

2CORZ ↑59 Watch$28.02 +0.83%BULLISH SWEEPCALL33$101KOPENING188167PULLBACK33
3TEVA ↑50 Watch$34.13 -0.25%BULLISH SWEEPCALL33$270KOPENING161258STRONG22
4TLT ↑50 Watch$87.58 +0.22%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNOT_FOUND—97STRONG99
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape has only 5 resolved trades out of 285 flagged - far below the 20-trade minimum needed for any verdict - so the 100% win rate and 200% avg return are statistically meaningless noise, and neither the monthly cohort averages nor the backtest Sharpe of 2.39 can be confirmed or contradicted yet.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 285 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Three signals today, all Watch tier and all bullish sweeps - a thin, low-conviction batch. CORZ leads at 64, the only name approaching the 65 threshold, with a bullish sweep pattern that at least has EMA alignment implied by its score. TLT at 55 and COIN at 50 are marginal reads with no signal above 65 and nothing at or above 80. The score distribution is compressed and bottom-heavy: median 55, max 64, zero names in the high-conviction bins. Macro regime sits NEUTRAL at 54.9, which neither confirms nor kills a bullish flow read but offers no tailwind. If forced to act, CORZ first at low size given it is the only name near the scoring threshold, but this batch is weak and patience is the correct default.

🧠 Ranks premium-selling candidates by the IV30 vs HV30 spread adjusted for Sinclair sector biases and value factors, where structural overpricing of implied vol creates a short-volatility edge.
Construction
IV30 vs HV30 spread + sector bias (Sinclair Table 4.7: Utilities +31%, Tech -8%) + value factors (P/E, RoE, mcap). Score 0-100 ranks premium-selling candidates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record of 56.8% win rate and +2.93% average return beating SPY by 2.3 points confirms the backtest Sharpe of 2.34 is not decaying, and the May 2026 cohort at 57.8% win rate and +4.48% average is the strongest monthly print in the window, so the tape is consistent with the thesis.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PCG at 78 and NEE at 77 lead a batch dominated by regulated utilities, with PM at 76 the lone consumer-staples name in the top eight. The score distribution is compressed and top-heavy: 39 of 42 signals clear 65, the max is only 78, and nothing reaches 80, so conviction differentiation within the batch is thin. Pattern data is null across every pick, leaving the entire case resting on the IV/HV spread and Sinclair's utility sector bias of +31%. NEUTRAL macro at 54.9 is not hostile to premium selling but adds no directional tailwind. Start with PCG at low-to-moderate conviction given the top score and utility sector alignment, size small, and wait for a pattern trigger before adding.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

IVZ and PATH both hit 100 with Very Strong tier, making them the lead names in an otherwise pattern-blind batch where no ticker carries a named setup. GPOR and RDDT follow at 99, but with no pattern flags on any of the eight names, the signal is purely vol-regime placement. The score distribution is top-heavy: 26 of 50 signals clear 80 and the median lands exactly at 80, so the batch is not thin, but it is bunched at the high end with little dispersion to differentiate. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.9), which neither endorses nor opposes the long bias here. IVZ first on vol-coherence grounds at moderate conviction, sized conservatively until a pattern or catalyst context fills in.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch is analytically flat: all 50 signals score exactly 100, median 100, every name clears the 80 threshold, so the score is functioning as a binary pass/fail gate rather than a ranking tool and intra-batch differentiation is impossible on score alone. Pattern data is null across the board, which strips the only other axis of comparison. Among the 8 shown names, ARCC and BTG are the most legible in context - ARCC as a large liquid BDC where put hedging by rate-sensitive holders has a plausible narrative, BTG as a gold miner where options activity maps cleanly to macro positioning. NEUTRAL macro at 54.9 neither supports nor opposes the contrarian long thesis. Preference is ARCC first, low conviction, pending any pattern or flow detail that separates it from the other 49 identical-score names.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record (65% WR, avg +2.0% vs SPY -1.6%, verdict KEEP) broadly confirms the backtest Sharpe of 1.10, but the monthly cohort trend is deteriorating, with win rates falling from 67% in March to 50.8% in May and avg return compressing from +4.9% to +1.0%, which signals the strategy's edge is thinning as the tape normalizes off the volatility spike.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 65% · avg +2.0% · beats SPY -1.6% by +3.6 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 492 resolved at +20d · WR 65% · avg +2.0% · vs SPY -1.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 492 closed · WR TB 39% · 23% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

GBTG and JAZZ both print 100 with LAUNCH patterns at the Very strong tier, making them the top two names from today's 50-signal batch. INTT and POWL follow at 95, also Very strong LAUNCH setups. The score distribution is strikingly compressed: every single signal scores 80 or above with a median of 85, meaning there is no low-conviction filler dragging the cohort down, but also no differentiation at the top beyond raw score gaps. A NEUTRAL macro regime (54.9) neither supports nor fights a reversal play, so the structural setup has to carry the trade. One caution worth flagging from the live tier data: Very strong has historically averaged only +0.4% at 62.5% win rate in the 90-day window, well below Strong's +4.1% at 81.5%, so the perfect scores on GBTG and JAZZ do not guarantee the best outcomes. Lead with JAZZ at moderate conviction given the sector and score, and treat POWL as the second name given its industrial exposure fits the current macro posture.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MNDY, NIO, and PDD lead the batch tied at 94, all flagged Very strong, but no pattern data is present for any name, which limits single-name conviction. The score distribution is compressed almost to a point: median 91, max 94, all 25 signals clear the 80 threshold, meaning the screener is not discriminating today, it is just printing a near-uniform top tier. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.9), which neither supports nor undercuts a mean-reversion contrarian thesis but removes any macro tailwind. If forced to act, MNDY gets first look over NIO and PDD given it is a US-listed name without the regulatory and custody risk of Chinese ADRs, but conviction is moderate at best in a flat macro tape with no pattern confirmation on any pick.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Identifies vol-of-vol climax events via VVIX percentile and VIX term structure to flag optimal sell-volatility entries at peak market fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑61 Watch$737.42 +0.57%SELL VOL1.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).

🧠 Detects institutional options sweeps at 5x normal volume with premium above $50K, filtered for calm, non-extended tickers to isolate informed flow over momentum chasing.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SMH prints the top score at 79 and Signal tier, but the pattern is a BEARISH SWEEP against a LONG direction label - that contradiction means someone is buying put protection or fading semis, not initiating a clean long, and this name deserves caution rather than a buy. PCG at 65 is the cleanest setup in the batch: Signal tier, unambiguous BULLISH SWEEP, and a utility name that fits a NEUTRAL macro regime (54.9) where defensive rotation has logic. The score distribution is weak - 14 signals, median 60, zero above 80, only 2 at or above 65 - this batch is flat and compressed with no standout conviction. PCG is first look at moderate conviction given the clean sweep and tier; everything else sits in Watch territory with undifferentiated scores in the low 60s.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLC and XLE are the only names at score 80 (Strong tier, LONG), separating cleanly from a flat cluster of five sectors sitting at the median of 55. The distribution is top-heavy and narrow: 4 of 11 signals reach 80 or above, the rest pile at or below the median, and pattern fields are null across the board, stripping out the signal detail that would normally differentiate. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.9, providing no directional tailwind to lean into the full group. First preference is XLC at 80, low conviction, small size only, given the thin pattern data and a macro setup that does not support aggressive sector rotation bets.

🧠 Flags stocks down 40%-plus from 52-week highs showing capitulation volume plus a reversal candle, scoring depth, ATR contraction, and volume spike to time mean-reversion entries.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record (WR 44%, avg +1.3% versus SPY -0.4%) barely clears the index and sits well below the backtest's 63% win rate, and the tier ordering is inverted in a way that matters - the Strong tier is running -9.7% average at 28.6% WR while the Signal tier is outperforming at +7.2% average, which directly contradicts the scoring logic and is the key reason to stay cautious on the middle of today's list.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ADCT at a perfect 100 and OCS at 91 are the two names worth leading with, both Very strong tier and both printing a breakout pattern on top of the deep-oversold setup, which is the combination this screener is designed to catch. FUTU, NBP, and WW cluster at 87 in the Strong tier but live tracking data shows the Strong cohort has been running a -9.7% avg return over 90 days, so they deserve skepticism today. Score distribution is broad rather than top-heavy: all 50 signals clear 65 but only 10 breach 80 and the median sits at 74, meaning the bulk of the batch is signal-grade filler. NEUTRAL macro at 54.9 offers no directional tailwind for a bounce strategy, so sizing should stay modest. ADCT first, small, given the perfect score and the screener's WATCH-level live performance.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks by annualized exponential-regression slope times R-squared over 90 days, filtering for clean uptrends above MA100 when SPY holds its MA200.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

EOD
Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Detects post-earnings continuation by combining EPS surprise magnitude, gap size, gap-hold behavior, and analyst revision activity to score stocks likely to drift higher over 1-3 weeks.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BBCP $BKE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BBCP ↑90●●● Very strong$11.36 +1.75%500.0%437.6%
2BKE ↑70● Signal$44.39 -0.40%24.7%4-3.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape confirms a real short-window edge, with 64% win rate and a +4.9% average 20-day return beating SPY by 4.7 points, which is consistent with the backtest's marginal verdict at h=5d and more than enough to justify keeping the screener active - the key number is that 4.7-point spread over SPY across 495 resolved trades, though the May 2026 cohort (n=621, 62% WR) dwarfs April (n=6, 50%) and the tier rankings are scrambled, with Watch outperforming Very strong on both WR and average return, which undermines tier-based position sizing.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires contrarian SPY longs when two or more major intermarket pair correlations break by 2-sigma or more over seven days, scaling severity with dislocation count.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record (WR 63.4%, avg plus 4.6%, beats SPY by plus 5.2 pts on a SPY reading of minus 0.6%) and the May 2026 cohort (WR 63%, avg plus 5.6%) together confirm the backtest Sharpe of roughly 0.96, but the key number is the Signal-tier avg of plus 9.3% (81.25% WR) versus Very strong at plus 2.77% (56.1% WR), which means today's all-Very-strong batch is drawing from the weakest-performing live tier.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals score exactly 100 with a median of 100, meaning the screener is fully saturated and offers zero intra-batch differentiation today. AMZN and BA are the highest-profile names in the top picks, and AMLP adds energy-sector exposure via MLP wrapper, but every name shares the same 100 score, Very strong tier, and null pattern field, so there is no data-driven reason to rank one above another. The batch lands entirely in Very strong tier, which is the underperforming tier in live tracking (56.1% WR, plus 2.77% avg) versus Signal tier (81.25% WR, plus 9.3% avg), a meaningful structural headwind. Macro at NEUTRAL (54.9) provides no directional lift. Take AMZN first if forced to pick, but size it small, because a fully ceiling-out score distribution with no pattern signal and a tier mismatch against the historically strongest cohort makes this a weak batch.

🧠 Detects stocks with short interest above 20% of float, a cost-to-borrow spike, and a price breakout above a key moving average, betting on forced short covering.
Construction
High short interest (>20% float) + cost-to-borrow spike + technical breakout above key MA. Score blends SI %, days-to-cover, CTB, and price momentum.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape confirms a genuine edge - 65.4% win rate and +8.0% avg over 20 days versus SPY at +0.3% - but the monthly cohorts cut against the headline: April averaged -0.7% with a 44% win rate, and May recovered to only +4.0%, both well short of the 90-day figure, suggesting the recent regime is compressing realized returns.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BEAM is the standout at a score of 90 (Very strong, PRIMED), the only name at the screener ceiling today. APGE and BHVN both sit at 85 (Strong, PRIMED) and are the logical follow-ons. The batch is top-heavy but not thin: 13 of 50 names score 80 or above, median lands at 70, and 47 of 50 clear 65, so signal concentration is real without being crowded at the top. Macro registers NEUTRAL at 54.9, which offers no squeeze-accelerating tailwind and raises the bar for timing. Start with BEAM at moderate conviction, APGE second.

🧠 Ranks stocks by relative volume across three lookback windows multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum, then percentile-scores the result to surface the tape's biggest volume-momentum leaders.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 Detects institutional OTM options block prints over $200K premium on quiet names, using next-day OI delta to confirm new accumulation versus position exits.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

30AIVI ↑53 Watch$55.71 -2.53%1.593$0.2M
31FFIC ↑52 Watch$15.47 -3.13%24.453$35.9M
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (57.2% WR, avg +3.6%, +2.8 pts over SPY) and the monthly cohorts (April avg +8.8%, May avg +4.5%) confirm the edge is real but decelerating, with the declining monthly average being the single number to watch.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Catches PEAD continuation entries: earnings gap-up, then a weekly red-candle pullback within 4 weeks, confirmed by a weekly close above the prior week's high.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5
  • Dollar-volume ≥ $50M
Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Flags daily rejection-tail bars at least 1.8x ATR formed at Market Profile value-area extremes, exploiting price returning toward fair value after failed breakouts.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.

Data confidence STARTER prod-cabled KVO+FORCE combo, paper KEPT WR 67% vs SKIPPED 46% over 60d; no formal Bonferroni verdict yet Full methodology →
🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW setups by removing any signal where KVO momentum cross or FORCE index flip is active within a 3-day window, using volume divergence as a negative gate.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $TLT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1TLT ↑50 Watch$87.58 +0.22%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNOT_FOUND—97STRONG99
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin and low-conviction: two signals, both Watch-tier, both BULLISH SWEEP, with a max score of 55 and a median of 50, and zero names clearing the 65 threshold. TLT at 55 is the marginal leader, a long-duration Treasury ETF catching a bullish sweep in a NEUTRAL macro regime (54.9) where rate-sensitive longs carry some logic as a risk-off hedge. COIN at 50 adds nothing differentiated, a crypto proxy with a sweep pattern but no score edge over TLT and no macro tailwind in a neutral tape. The score distribution is compressed at the floor, which the filter was designed to prevent, suggesting the underlying SMART_FLOW universe is itself quiet today. If forced to act, TLT first at low size only, given the regime score barely clears neutral and neither name earns conviction above Watch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape confirms the screener rather than contradicts it: 90-day tracking shows 53.4% win rate and a +5.4-point edge over SPY, but the tier split is the key number - Watch names run at a -6.3% average, so the entire edge is concentrated in Very Strong and Strong tiers, which aligns with the backtest direction even if the formal backtest was rejected on statistical grounds.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MUU is the only name worth discussing at a perfect score of 100 in the Very Strong tier, a clean separation from the rest of the batch. AGL is a distant second at 79 in Signal, and pattern data is null for both, which limits conviction on setup quality. The score distribution is severely top-heavy: 36 signals, median score of 15, only 1 name above 80 and 2 above 65 - the bulk of the batch is noise. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.9), which does not actively support breakout momentum plays. Preference is MUU first and only, with moderate conviction, given the barren setup quality beneath it.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record superficially supports use (54% WR, avg +2.6% vs SPY +1.3%), but the tier breakdown is a red flag: Strong-tier picks, exactly the tier of today's two trigger names, produced a 20% WR and a -20.8% avg over 10 trades, meaning the highest-conviction signals have historically squeezed rather than reverted.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DD leads today at score 90 with a BREAKDOWN pattern, the lone Very Strong print and the only name where the move appears to have already rolled. AMC and BTQ both print 80 with TRIGGER, signalling the climax is live but not yet cracked. The score distribution skews middle-heavy: 35 of 50 signals cluster at 65 or above with a median of 65, only 3 names clear 80, so the batch is not thin but also not top-heavy in a meaningful way. NEUTRAL macro at 54.9 is an uninspiring backdrop for aggressive shorts. Given the live data shows Strong-tier picks (the tier containing AMC and BTQ) ran only 20% WR over 10 trades, first preference goes to DD at highest score with confirmed breakdown, low conviction.

RIOT and OSCR are the most recognizable names at score 100, tier Very strong, but with pattern fields null there is no structural context behind either rank. 🧠 Flags deeply oversold equities via weekly RSI(14) below 30 then confirms daily reversal with candlestick and zone scoring for mean-reversion longs.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The tape confirms the KILL verdict, not contradicts it: the 90-day avg of +4.0% trails SPY by 2.9 points, and the monthly cohort trend is deteriorating sharply from +6.19% avg in March to +0.40% in May, even as win rate holds near 70%.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 413 closed · WR TB 60% · 23% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NUS leads at 78 with a live TRIGGER pattern in Signal tier, making it the single name worth watching first given it sits at the batch ceiling. VERI follows at 76, also TRIGGER, also Signal - two active triggers at the top of a 50-name field where nothing clears 80 and only 5 names reach 65 is a thin distribution, not a strong pulse. DDL and DLNG are SETUP-stage at 72 and 70 respectively, meaning the catalyst has not fired yet and require patience. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.9, which neither aids nor kills a contrarian RSI bounce strategy but removes any tailwind argument. Conviction is low-to-moderate: NUS first, sized small, given the doctrine REJECT on backtest and a batch with no 80-plus scores.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, OI delta, and premium spikes on crypto ETFs to front-run spot crypto momentum through regulated equity wrappers.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CLSK and HUT both print 85 Strong-tier TRIGGER scores, the only two names above 65 in a six-signal batch, making this a top-heavy day with a hard drop to 60 at the median. The catch: Strong tier in the 90-day live window carries a 0% win rate and a -13.2% average 20-day return across its one resolved trade, and Signal tier is worse at -20.2% average across eight. Watch-tier names - CORZ and MARA at 60 - belong to the only tier with positive live returns (+5.7% avg, 76% win rate). Macro is neutral at 54.9, neither a tailwind nor a headwind for beta-heavy miners. Preference is MARA over CORZ at low conviction given Watch-tier historical edge, not CLSK or HUT despite the louder score.

ARKG leads at 76, the only name with meaningful separation from the pack, reflecting genomics exposure at a moment when healthcare broadly clusters near the top - VHT and XLV both print 70. IFRA at 72 and SMIN at 71 add infrastructure and India small-cap angle, though both lack pattern confirmation. Score distribution is compressed and underwhelming: zero names clear 80, only 8 of 50 reach 65, and the median sits at 56, meaning the bulk of the universe is generating noise. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.9 provides no directional cover. Take ARKG first if forced, low conviction, given it leads a weak batch with no pattern data to anchor the trade.

The score distribution is almost meaningless as a filter today: all 50 signals clear 80 and the median sits at 94, meaning the screener is delivering a compressed, top-heavy pile with almost no differentiation between the bottom and top of the list. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.9 does not argue against insider buys but removes any tailwind, so sizing should stay measured. Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 35% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names hit the maximum score of 100 today - BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM - all tagged Very strong LONG, but pattern data is null across every pick, stripping out any setup-level differentiation. Score distribution is top-heavy: 41 of 50 signals sit at 65 or above, with the entire top cluster piled at 100, leaving little gradation to rank names against each other. The macro regime at NEUTRAL (54.9) neither supports nor blocks these longs, but it argues for sizing discipline rather than full conviction. The most important wrinkle is that 90-day tracking shows Very strong names averaging only +3.4%, well below the Watch tier at +9.2%, so today's perfect-score batch is not the best-performing cohort in live data. First look goes to CELH and BL for liquidity and name quality, but at reduced size until pattern fields populate and allow real differentiation.

🧠 Aggregates quarterly 13F position changes from 20 top hedge funds into a conviction score, surfacing consensus institutional accumulation via weighted new and increased positions.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

Take ASML first at moderate conviction, but price this data as 60-90 days stale before sizing.

🧠 Finds individual stocks lagging their outperforming sector, scoring by the product of sector relative strength and stock under-performance to capture delayed catch-up moves.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks the cheapest US equities by EV/EBIT (Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple), with score 100 signaling the lowest multiple in the universe that day.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The tape offers nothing useful: 0 of 37 signals have resolved, the only data point is a 33% win rate on 3 tiebreaker observations, and monthly cohorts are empty, so neither the backtest nor the live record can confirm or contradict each other.

HALF SIZEinsufficient data (0 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 37 signals captured
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 5 closed · WR TB 20% · 0% expired · sample too small to read (N<10)
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ leads at a perfect score of 100 and WRD follows at 97, both in the Very Strong tier, making them the two names to focus on first. GPC at 90 adds a large-cap, more liquid option in the same tier. The distribution is top-heavy: 7 of 16 signals clear 80 and the median sits at 73, meaning today's batch has genuine spread between the top names and the rest rather than a flat cluster. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.9, which does not actively support deep value mean-reversion but does not block it either. Given the absence of any pattern tags and a screener with no completed backtest, treat this as a watch list, not a buy list - if forced to pick one name, BZ at 100 gets first look at the smallest position size.

🧠 Detects clusters of 3 or more insiders buying the same stock within 30 days, weighting C-suite and director purchases more heavily.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record shows 60% win rate and +3.6% average 20-day return versus SPY at +1.3%, a clean +2.2 point edge that earns the KEEP verdict, but the April cohort at 44% win rate and +1.2% average is a clear warning that performance degrades when the broader tape is rough, so the edge is real but regime-sensitive.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)

🧠 Detects bull traps where price breaks above value area high then closes back inside, signaling trapped longs and a short entry.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $AAXJ $AVEM $ARTY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1AAXJ ↓75● Signal$120.08 +2.46%BULL_TRAP118.4297.83
2AVEM ↓75● Signal$96.94 +2.28%BULL_TRAP96.8081.69
3ARTY ↓70● Signal$76.64 +3.63%BULL_TRAP75.8371.57
4ASX ↓70● Signal$42.40 +2.56%BULL_TRAP41.3435.16
5ADI ↓65● Signal$430.80 +4.27%BULL_TRAP431.49416.05
6AIRR ↓65● Signal$132.62 +1.64%BULL_TRAP130.81124.76
7AIS ↓65● Signal$85.60 +6.64%BULL_TRAP82.4280.90
8ANET ↓65● Signal$167.32 +3.45%BULL_TRAP167.51144.39
9ARW ↓65● Signal$230.11 +2.38%BULL_TRAP230.42189.83
10ASHR ↓65● Signal$36.83 +2.70%BULL_TRAP36.3634.81
11ALAB ↓60 Watch$419.74 +4.96%BULL_TRAP416.67340.37
12AME ↓60 Watch$238.67 +1.40%BULL_TRAP238.90232.78
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Ranks macro ETFs by Antonacci dual momentum: positive 12-month absolute return plus top relative rank among peers, going long the leaders.
Construction
Antonacci dual momentum on a macro ETF set: absolute (12m return > 0) AND relative (top rank vs peers). Long the leaders.

Post-screener curation: combines UNUSUAL_V3 + SMART_FLOW + BTFD + BOTTOM signals with insider/congress overlay. Conviction score 0-10, exclusion of gamma hedging and ETF hedges.

🧠 Captures top relative-strength names inside leading sectors by combining 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster rank into a composite score.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$24.67 +1.23%Real Estate19.0%31.5%
2AAMI ↑76● Signal$71.32 +1.34%Financials9.2%52.1%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 runs more than 1.5 sigma above HV20 on a 90-day window, identifying crowded fear premiums as candidates for options selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $NFLX $ALNY $CMCSA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1NFLX ↓64 Watch$71.28 -0.78%46%28%1.62FEAR
2ALNY ↓61 Watch$292.31 -0.59%49%32%1.55FEAR
3CMCSA ↓61 Watch$22.68 +0.15%40%26%1.53FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day resolved tape shows a 50.7% win rate and +4.8% average 20-day return versus SPY at +2.4%, which earns the KEEP verdict, but the monthly cohort trend is a yellow flag - April's average of +10.8% deteriorated to +3.0% in May, a 7.8-point step-down that suggests the signal's edge is compressing with the calmer tape.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put/call skew spikes versus 90-day baseline as a contrarian long signal when the underlying is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $GOOGL $MU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1GOOGL ↓100●●● Very strong$336.37 -2.58%151%94—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
2MU ↑64 Watch$1251.20 +19.33%38%1—BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape at WR 60% and avg +11.7% - beating SPY by 9.9 points across 70 resolved trades - confirms usable edge, and the monthly cohort trend strengthens that case, with May's WR jumping to 67.4% and avg of +14.5% versus April's 47.4% and +9.2%, suggesting the signal is getting sharper as the sample grows.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV overwhelms call IV at extreme percentiles, detecting unusual institutional put demand as a potential bearish-positioning signal.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$385.95 +1.01%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Seven resolved trades show a 71.4% win rate that looks acceptable on the surface, but the avg 20-day return of -0.33% trails SPY by roughly 1.77 points, and the key number is the single Strong-tier trade that lost 88.8%, which drags the cohort average and reveals that tier classification, not win rate, is the real risk discriminant - a pattern the thin monthly data (May +14.6% avg, April -20.2% avg) does not yet resolve.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV sits below Yang-Zhang HV20-implied expected move, targeting vol underpricing before the event.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $MU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1MU ↓50 Watch$1251.20 +19.33%REVERSAL1DOWN (reversal)BUY PUT (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is as thin as it gets: one signal, MU at score 50 on a Watch tier with no pattern tagged and a SHORT direction that lacks any supporting context from the screener output. A median of 50 with zero names clearing 65 means the screener found nothing worth acting on. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.9 adds no tailwind for a vol-buying thesis. Pass on MU today, the data is too sparse to assign any real conviction.

Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $BTU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1BTU ↓56 Watch$23.16 -0.56%GREED0.56-2.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects false breakouts beyond Value Area boundaries where trapped shorts or longs fuel a mean-reversion snap-back.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AAP
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AAP ↑55 Watch$58.39 +0.78%BEAR_TRAP54.7958.632.77
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape looks eye-catching at 85.7% win rate and +16.5% avg vs SPY's +0.52%, but 7 resolved trades against a need of 20 makes that number statistically meaningless, and the Time-Based tracker at 39.3% win rate on 28 signals contradicts the resolved-trade optimism, so the verdict stays WATCH and the backtest remains unbacked.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=2 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and institutional flow to identify options edge setups.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓86●● Strong$22.99 -2.95%56%257%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Altman

ALTMANEOD⚠ Dormant · 2026-05-23 (21d)
Live: base version (no variants documented)
Today's best

0 signals ≥65 · 0 shown
Data confidence
STARTER · untested
no backtest stats yet
Refresh
EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens for financial distress risk using Altman Z-score derived factors to flag deteriorating credit quality.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54.9) is not the constraint here, the screener itself found nothing actionable. Pass on this batch entirely.

🧠 STFR screens for a specific pattern via proprietary logic; source script defines the exact mechanism and edge.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

🧠 VSA (Volume Spread Analysis) detects institutional accumulation or distribution by comparing price spread and close location against volume magnitude.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (54.9), which is not hostile to VSA setups, but the screener found nothing worth naming. No actionable preference today - pass on this batch entirely.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
